name: Cream of Cauliflower Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 large yellow onion, sliced very thin
1 large cauliflower (2-1/2 to 3 lbs.), cut into florets
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add onion and lower the heat to medium-low. Sprinkle with 1/4 teaspoon of salt (if desired) and cook, stirring frequently until caramel brown in color, about 12 minutes.
3. Add cauliflower and cook for 3 minutes, stirring often.
4. Add flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
5. Add broth and bay leaf. Bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat to simmer, partially cover, and cook until the cauliflower is tender, about 10 minutes.
7. Remove and discard the bay leaf. Reserve about 1/4 cup of cooked vegetables for garnish.
8. Transfer soup in batches to a blender or food processor and puree until smooth. Return to the pan.
9. Stir in milk, salt, and pepper. Reheat until hot but not boiling, about 3 minutes.
10. Divide garnish vegetables among 4 bowls and pour soup over top. Serve hot, sprinkled with nutmeg.
Serves 4.
